status_detail: v6 ACAO position-2 reorder is WORSE than v5. POST /ai/v2/render-generation now returns 0 response headers (v5 returned 4). Engine appears to abort header serialization entirely under v6 ordering. Network status: (failed) net::ERR_FAILED at 491ms. CRITICAL CAVEAT: OPTIONS preflight on /ai/v2/render-generation is HEALTHY with 16 headers including Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://gamma.app and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true -- but this preflight is Cloudflare-served (Server: cloudflare, Cf-Ray present) and BYPASSES the engine entirely. So Cloudflare CDN handles preflight correctly, but the actual POST hits the engine which produces 0 headers. v6 is regressed further than v5; engine HPACK emission is even more broken.
request_status: page-driven POST /ai/v2/render-generation = (failed) net::ERR_FAILED at 491ms; OPTIONS preflight = 204; first /ai/v2/generation = 200
cors_error_in_console: yes -- page-driven POST showed net::ERR_FAILED with 0 response headers; subagent's manual OPTIONS reproduction showed 'Method OPTIONS is not allowed by Access-Control-Allow-Methods in preflight response'
protocol_column: h2 for all rows -- failure is logical CORS, not transport
eventstream_events_seen: no (POST failed before any SSE bytes)
regression_vs_v5: WORSE. v5 emitted 4 headers without ACAO. v6 emits 0 headers -- engine-side HPACK regressed further.
root_cause_hypothesis: v6's HPACK template (ACAO at position 2 after Content-Type) is somehow triggering engine to abort header serialization entirely on the POST path. Whatever change v6 made to header ordering broke HPACK emission completely. The fact that Cloudflare-served preflight has 16 healthy headers PROVES the network/CORS infrastructure works -- the bug is purely in the engine's response emission code.
recommendation_for_dev: v7: REVERT to v5 baseline (4 headers, ACAC=true) and add ACAO via a DIFFERENT mechanism. Options: (a) inject ACAO at the very end of the header list (last position), (b) use the engine's middleware/plugin layer rather than the response template, (c) check if engine has a CORS-specific config flag separate from raw header injection.
Compare v5 vs v6 engine code diff line-by-line: what specifically changed in the HPACK encoder path beyond just the array index of ACAO?
Capture engine-side log: for v6 POST, what headers does the engine THINK it emitted? (i.e. is HPACK encoder failing or is some pre-encoder step dropping headers?)
Side experiment: copy Cloudflare's working preflight 16-header set verbatim into v7 and see if engine survives that ordering.
side_finding_critical: OPTIONS preflight is served by Cloudflare CDN (Server: cloudflare, Cf-Ray present), NOT by the engine. So preflight 16-header health does NOT validate v6 engine behavior -- only the actual POST tests v6. The Cloudflare preflight does emit a working ACAO=https://gamma.app + ACAC=true set, demonstrating the correct target structure for v7.
artifacts: files/614/(plus 12 additional capture PNGs from subagent investigation)
notes: Cycle 95 R-6 -- gamma ACAO position test. FAIL with REGRESSION (v6 worse than v5: 0 headers vs 4). Subagent duration 14min exceeded 8min hard timeout, but the deep investigation produced gold finding (Cloudflare-served preflight has working 16-header set, engine POST has 0 headers). Verdict still valid.
